questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text: The melting point of a chemical species is  \( 146^{\circ} \) C. It is soluble in water and the solution formed does not conduct electricity. Which of the following structures would this chemical species have?

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
Consider the following experimental set-up :
[IMG:q2_stem_0.jpg]

Which of the following statements is correct when an electric current passes through the circuit?

options.label: A
options.text: Blue colour is observed in the dilute  \( H_{2}SO_{4}(aq) \)  around Y.

options.label: B
options.text: Gas bubbles are observed in the dilute  \( H_{2}SO_{4}(aq) \)  around \( Y_{.} \)

options.label: C
options.text: Orange colour is observed in the dilute  \( H_{2}SO_{4}(aq) \)

options.label: D
options.text: Electrons flow from X to Y through the external circuit.

questions.subject: Chemistry
topics: Topic 5: Fossil Fuels and Carbon Compounds
questions.question_text: Which of the following statements is INCORRECT?

options.label: A
options.text: Cracking of heavy oil can give ethene.

options.label: B
options.text: Electrolysis of sea water can give chlorine.

options.label: C
options.text: Strong heating of limestone can give oxygen.

options.label: D
options.text: Fractional distillation of liquefied air can give nitrogen.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text: M, Q and R are three different metals. When their oxides are separately heated, only the oxide of M gives a metallic lustre. When their carbonates are separately heated with a Bunsen burner, only the carbonate of R gives no observable changes. Which of the following shows the increasing order of reactivity of the metals?

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text: 15.0 cm³ of 0.20 M Ba(NO₃)₂(aq) is added to 25.0 cm³ of 0.10 M Na₂SO₄(aq). After the reaction is completed, which of the following ions has the highest concentration in the mixture?

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
Refer to the information in the table below :
SolutionContentspH
X50 cm³ of 0.001 M HCl(aq)3.0
Y25 cm³ of 0.001 M H₂SO₄(aq)2.7
Z50 cm³ of 0.1 M CH₃COOH(aq)2.9
Which of the following statements is correct?

options.label: A
options.text: X has a higher pH than Z because HCl is a stronger acid than CH₃COOH.

options.label: B
options.text: Y has a lower pH than X because the volume of  \( H_{2}SO_{4}(aq) \)  is smaller than that of HCl(aq).

options.label: C
options.text: Y has a lower pH than X because  \( H_{2}SO_{4} \)  is a strong dibasic acid but HCl is a strong monobasic acid.

options.label: D
options.text: Y has a lower pH than Z because the concentration of  \( H_{2}SO_{4}(aq) \)  is lower than that of  \( CH_{3}COOH(aq) \) .

questions.subject: Chemistry
topics: Topic 7: Redox Reactions, Chemical Cells and Electrolysis
questions.question_text: The oxidation number of Pb in  \( \mathrm{Pb}_{10}(\mathrm{VO}_{4})_{6}\mathrm{F}_{2} \)  is +2. What is the oxidation number of V?

questions.subject: Chemistry
topics: Topic 5: Fossil Fuels and Carbon Compounds
questions.question_text:
Consider two compounds with their structures shown below :
[IMG:q8_stem_0.jpg]  [IMG:q8_stem_1.jpg]  [IMG:q8_stem_2.jpg]

Which of the following statements is correct?

options.label: C
options.text: They belong to the same homologous series.

options.label: D
options.text: Both of them can decolourise bromine solution in the dark.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text: Gases discharged from coal-fired power plants contain  \( SO_{2} \) .  \( SO_{2} \)  is also regarded as an air pollutant. What is the most suitable way to remove the  \( SO_{2} \)  before discharging these gases into the atmosphere?

options.label: A
options.text: Pass these gases through calcium oxide.

options.label: B
options.text: Pass these gases through concentrated sulphuric acid.

options.label: C
options.text: Cool these gases to liquefy  \( SO_{2} \)  for subsequent removal.

options.label: D
options.text: Pass these gases through an organic solvent such as hexane.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text: Which of the following processes involves the breaking of hydrogen bonds?

questions.subject: Chemistry
topics: Topic 5: Fossil Fuels and Carbon Compounds
questions.question_text: The monosubstitution of methane with chlorine under diffuse sunlight involves several steps. Which of the following steps initiates the reaction?

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
Given that :
\( 2\mathrm{Mg(s)} + \mathrm{O}_{2}(\mathrm{~g}) \rightarrow 2\mathrm{MgO(s)} \)   \( \Delta H_{1} \)
\( \mathrm{Mg(s)} + \frac{1}{2}\mathrm{O}_{2}(\mathrm{~g}) + \mathrm{CO}_{2}(\mathrm{~g}) \rightarrow \mathrm{MgCO}_{3}(\mathrm{~s}) \)   \( \Delta H_{2} \)
\( \mathrm{MgCO}_{3}(\mathrm{~s}) \rightarrow \mathrm{MgO}(\mathrm{s}) + \mathrm{CO}_{2}(\mathrm{~g}) \)   \( \Delta H_{3} \)

What is  \( \Delta H_{3} \)  ?

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
W, X, Y and Z, each represents one of the following solutions:
\(HCl(aq)\)   \(NaOH(aq)\)   \(MgCl_{2}(aq)\)   \(Na_{2}CO_{3}(aq)\)

Given that :
• Mixing W and X gives a white precipitate.
• Mixing W and Y gives a white precipitate.
• Mixing W and Z gives a clear colourless solution.

What is Z?

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
Based on the experimental set-up in the diagram below, after 8.0 g of sodium nitrate solid is completely dissolved in 50 cm\( ^{3} \) of water, the temperature drops by 6\({}^{\circ} \)C.
[IMG:q14_stem_0.jpg]

Which of the following would give a drop of temperature by  \( 3^{\circ} \)C under the same experimental conditions?

options.label: A
options.text: After 2.0 g of sodium nitrate solid is completely dissolved in 25 cm \( ^{3} \) of water.

options.label: B
options.text: After 4.0 g of sodium nitrate solid is completely dissolved in 100 cm \( ^{3} \) of water.

options.label: C
options.text: After 16.0 g of sodium nitrate solid is completely dissolved in 100 cm \( ^{3} \) of water.

options.label: D
options.text: After 24.0 g of sodium nitrate solid is completely dissolved in 75 cm \( ^{3} \) of water.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
When 7.89 g of carbon monoxide gas burns completely, 80 kJ of heat is released. Under those experimental conditions, the enthalpy change of formation of carbon dioxide gas is  \( -394\ kJ\ mol^{-1} \) . What is the enthalpy change of formation of carbon monoxide gas under the same experimental conditions?

(Relative atomic masses : C = 12.0, O = 16.0)

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text: A sample of sulphuric acid was completely neutralised by 25.0 cm³ of 0.200 M potassium hydroxide solution. The salt solution obtained was then made up to 100.0 cm³ with deionised water. What is the concentration of the resulting salt solution?

questions.subject: Chemistry
topics: Topic 5: Fossil Fuels and Carbon Compounds
questions.question_text: What is the systematic name of  \( CH_{2}BrCHBrCH_{2}CH_{2}I \) ?

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
Both aluminium and iron form oxides on their surfaces when they are exposed in air. The oxide of aluminium can prevent the aluminium from further corrosion, but the oxide of iron cannot prevent the iron from further corrosion. What is / are the reason(s)?

(1) The oxide of aluminium adheres firmly on the aluminium surface while the oxide of iron adheres loosely on the iron surface.
(2) The oxide of aluminium is insoluble in water while the oxide of iron is soluble in water.
(3) The oxide of aluminium has a giant ionic structure while the oxide of iron does not.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
The composition by mass of element X in the compound  \( K_{2}XO_{4} \)  is 26.8%. Which of the following statements concerning X is / are correct?

(1) X is a transition metal.
(2) X is an element in Group VI of the Periodic Table.
(3) X is an element in the fourth period of the Periodic Table.

(Relative atomic masses : O = 16.0, K = 39.1)

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
The structure of a portion of a polymer is shown below :
[IMG:q20_stem_0.jpg]

Which of the following statements concerning the polymer is / are correct?

(1) [IMG:q20_stem_1.jpg] is the repeating unit of it.

(2) [IMG:q20_stem_2.jpg] is a monomer of it.

(3) HOCH₂COOH is a monomer of it.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
Which of the following solids has / have delocalised electrons in its / their structure(s) ?

(1) graphite
(2) silicon
(3) silver

questions.subject: Chemistry
topics: Topic 7: Redox Reactions, Chemical Cells and Electrolysis
questions.question_text:
Which of the following statements concerning hydrogen-oxygen fuel cells are correct?

(1) When used to power vehicles, they are more environmentally friendly than using petrol engine.
(2) When used in space stations, they can produce drinking water in addition to energy.
(3) When used as a back-up power source in hospitals, they do not produce noise pollution.

questions.subject: Chemistry
topics: Topic 7: Redox Reactions, Chemical Cells and Electrolysis
questions.question_text:
Consider the following two electrolytic cells :
[IMG:q23_stem_0.jpg]   [IMG:q23_stem_1.jpg]
During electrolysis, which of the following would occur in Electrolytic cell 1 but not in Electrolytic cell 2?

(1) Gas bubbles are given out.
(2) The blue solution becomes paler.
(3) A reddish brown solid is deposited.

questions.subject: Chemistry
topics: Topic 12: Patterns in the Chemical World
questions.question_text:
1st statement : Iron(II) hydroxide is a base.
2nd statement : Iron(II) hydroxide is insoluble in water.

options.label: A
options.text: Both statements are true and the 2nd statement is a correct explanation of the 1st statement.

options.label: B
options.text: Both statements are true but the 2nd statement is NOT a correct explanation of the 1st statement.

options.label: C
options.text: The 1st statement is false but the 2nd statement is true.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
Direction: Questions 25 and 26 refer to the following experiment on the study of the rate of reaction between \( HCO_{2}H(aq) \) and \( Br_{2}(aq) \) at a certain temperature. It is given that the rate depends on both the concentrations of \( HCO_{2}H(aq) \) and \( Br_{2}(aq) \) : \( \mathrm{HCO_{2}H(aq)+Br_{2}(aq)\rightarrow2HBr(aq)+CO_{2}(g)} \) 5.0 cm³ of 0.05 M \( HCO_{2}H(aq) \) are separately added to four conical flasks each containing \( Br_{2}(aq) \) prepared by mixing different volumes of 0.05 M \( Br_{2}(aq) \) and water as shown in the table below:
Conical flaskVolume of 0.05 M \( \mathrm{Br}_{2}(\mathrm{aq})/\mathrm{cm}^{3} \)Volume of water / \( \mathrm{cm}^{3} \)
A1.04.0
B2.03.0
C3.02.0
D4.01.0
In which of the above conical flasks does the reaction have the fastest initial rate?

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text: Which of the following graphs best represents the variation of  \( [Br_{2}(aq)] \)  in the reaction mixture of conical flask B with time?

questions.subject: Chemistry
topics: Topic 7: Redox Reactions, Chemical Cells and Electrolysis
questions.question_text: Copper(II) oxide can catalyse the decomposition of hydrogen peroxide to form oxygen and water. In an experiment, hydrogen peroxide solution is shaken with copper(II) oxide in a test tube. What would be observed in the test tube after the completion of the reaction?

options.label: B
options.text: a blue solid and a colourless liquid

options.label: C
options.text: a black solid and a colourless liquid

options.label: D
options.text: a reddish brown solid and a colourless liquid

questions.subject: Chemistry
topics: Topic 12: Patterns in the Chemical World
questions.question_text: Which of the following statements correctly describes the property of an amphoteric oxide?

options.label: A
options.text: It can react as an acid or as a base.

options.label: B
options.text: It can react with water to form an acid and an alkali.

options.label: C
options.text: It can be simultaneously oxidised and reduced in a reaction.

options.label: D
options.text: It can react with water to form an oxidising agent and a reducing agent.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
Consider the following reaction :
\(  HOOCCH_{2}COCH_{2}CHO\xrightarrow{NaBH_{4}(aq)}Y \)

What is Y?

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
Consider the information shown in the table below :
[IMG:q30_stem_0.jpg]

Which of the following liquids have identical boiling point?

options.label: A
options.text: liquids in bottle A and bottle B only

options.label: B
options.text: liquids in bottle A and bottle C only

options.label: C
options.text: liquids in bottle B and bottle C only

options.label: D
options.text: liquids in bottle A, bottle B and bottle C

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
Consider the following reaction under certain conditions :
\( 2X_{3}(g)\rightleftharpoons3X_{2}(g)\qquad K_{c}=4.0mol dm^{-3} \)

The reaction quotient is 2.0 mol dm \( ^{-3} \)  at a certain moment. Which of the following statements is / are correct?

(1) The reaction quotient is larger than 2.0 mol dm\( ^{-3} \) after a period of time.
(2) The backward reaction is faster than the forward reaction at that moment.
(3) The concentration of  \( X_{2}(g) \)  must be equal to the concentration of  \( X_{3}(g) \)  at that moment.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
Consider the following equilibrium system :

\({HA(aq)\rightleftharpoons H^{+}(aq)+A^{-}(aq)}\quad\Delta H>0\)
Notice that \(HA(aq)\) and \(A^{-}\) are colourless and pink in colour respectively.

Which of the following statements is / are correct?

(1) Adding  \( Na_{2}CO_{3}(s) \)  would make its colour become paler.
(2) Increasing the temperature would make its colour become darker.
(3) Adding a few drops of concentrated HCl(aq) would increase the concentration of A\( ^{-} \) (aq).

questions.subject: Chemistry
topics: Topic 12: Patterns in the Chemical World
questions.question_text:
Which of the following statements concerning the elements in the third period of the Periodic Table going from Na to Cl is / are correct?

(1) The bond type of the elements changes from metallic bonding to covalent bonding.
(2) The oxide of the elements changes from acidic to basic.
(3) The electrical conductivity of the elements keeps decreasing.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
Which of the following mixtures would NOT separate into two liquid layers after heating under reflux for a period of time?

(1) HCOOCH₂CH₃(I) and excess NaOH(aq)
(2)  \( CH_{3}CH_{2}CH_{2}Cl(I) \)  and excess concentrated NaOH(aq)
(3)  \( CH_{3}CH_{2}CHO(I) \)  and excess acidified  \( K_{2}Cr_{2}O_{7}(aq) \)

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
The diagram below shows the structure of a compound.
[IMG:q35_stem_0.jpg]

Which of the following statements concerning the compound are correct?

(1) It has an amide group.
(2) Its structure has only one chiral carbon.
(3) It can be converted to an alcohol by using an appropriate reducing agent.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
1st statement : Methyl ethanoate and ethyl methanoate have similar chemical properties.
2nd statement : Methyl ethanoate and ethyl methanoate are isomers.

options.label: A
options.text: Both statements are true and the 2nd statement is a correct explanation of the 1st statement.

options.label: B
options.text: Both statements are true but the 2nd statement is NOT a correct explanation of the 1st statement.

options.label: C
options.text: The 1st statement is false but the 2nd statement is true.
